listBibl.head: Manuscript Descriptions
listBibl.bibl: Tabulae codicum manu scriptorum praeter graecos et orientales in Bibliotheca Palatina Vindobonensi asservatorum (photomech.Nachdr.der Ausgabe Wien 1870, Graz 1965) IV, S.58

projectDesc.p: The ARITHMETIC project investigates the transformation of mathematical knowledge and practice in late medieval Europe, specifically focusing on the critical transition from the Middle Ages to the Early Modern period. While the history of Latin-based mathematical theory is well-documented, the project aims to bridge a significant scholarly gap regarding how arithmetic spread through vernacular languages during the 15th and early 16th centuries. By analyzing German arithmetic treatises from their initial appearance around 1400 until the widespread availability of printed reckoning-books, the research tracks how mathematical language evolved from elite Latin traditions into common everyday use before the age of print.
